metadata.description: <FILL-IN: One-paragraph summary of what this package bundles and who consumes it.>
metadata.documentationUri: <FILL-IN: absolute URL to the per-feature docs/architecture/*.md file on the public repo>

toolRequirements.description: <FILL-IN: short description of what this CLI does>

publishing.marketplaceTarget: <FILL-IN: where this package publishes, e.g. 'github-releases' or omit>
publishing.sourceRepository: <FILL-IN: absolute URL of the source repo>
publishing.archiveFamily: <FILL-IN: asset family name, e.g. 'elegy-<your-feature>-wrapper' for wrappers or 'elegy-<your-feature>' for CLI binaries>
publishing.assetPrefix: <FILL-IN: asset prefix, usually same as archiveFamily>
publishing.skillBridge: <FILL-IN: relative path to SKILL.md mirror, omit for non-wrapper features>
publishing.installer: <FILL-IN: 'install.sh' for wrappers, omit for non-wrapper features>

extensions._template_instructions: 1. Copy this file to contracts/fixtures/elegy-plugin-package.<your-feature>.json. 2. Replace every <FILL-IN: ...> placeholder with real values; remove the `_template_instructions` extension after authoring. 3. Add the per-feature skill fixture to contracts/fixtures/skill.<your-feature>.json (use skill.minimal.json as a starting point). 4. Fill in capabilityProjections with one entry per CLI subcommand or tool, mapping to the actual binary surface. 5. Set hostPolicyHints.sideEffectClass to read_only | disk_read | disk_write | network_outbound | process_spawn per the actual capability. 6. Fill the publishing block: cratePath is the workspace crate name (e.g. 'elegy-memory'); assetKind is 'cli' or 'wrapper'; archiveFamily is the per-feature asset name; assetPrefix matches it; skillBridge and installer are required for wrapper assetKind. 7. Validate with: cargo run -p elegy-cli -- contracts validate --project . && cargo test -p elegy-contracts --test conformance -- --nocapture. 8. Add the per-feature DISTRIBUTION.md at rust/features/<your-feature>/DISTRIBUTION.md describing the binary's distribution shape; do not list the feature in a central catalog. 9. The central publish orchestrator (`.github/workflows/publish-orchestrator.yml`) auto-discovers the surface from this fixture; no per-feature workflow file is required. See contracts/AGENTS.md for the authority rules.
